item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Deliberate 2 year necropost because I came to point out this same contradiction.

Voct, fatal/non-fatal is a strict division. Since the prof was still alive the mauling was non-fatal by definition. Even if we assume he would have certainly died very soon; it would still be a non-fatal wound right up till the moment he actually croaks.

This hole in the plot is easily plugged by using less definitive adjectives in the description. If "major" wounds are healed but "minor" injuries remain, there is no issue.
The question could also be skipped entirely by just having all injuries heal on waking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do you know why he was paralyzed?

Do you know exactly what injuries he experienced? Which organs were ruptured? How long he would have lived if he hadn't been brought back to the bed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seems to contradict itself, between:

be transported to SCP-1587, which will then demanifest from SCP-1587-1 and remanifest within normal reality; this will also occur if the subject experiences an injury which would otherwise be fatal. All such injuries are healed upon the transition to normal reality; non-fatal injuries, however, are not.

and

it lunged at me, broke (?) my back. Was (felt?) paralyzed from waist down, […] Halfway to LZ, time elapsed; materialized back on bed, returned to bedroom. Back injuries seem totally healed.

I.e. at first the SCP claims that only fatal injuries are healed, but then he claims his partial paralysis was healed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, yours! Also, hi again! I've missed you! Really grateful! :)
I didn't know that you still checked this page? Or maybe you accessed it through recent posts… hmm…

See you around!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Um.

You're the original author of this article. Your version had sunk to -13 before I took over.

I don't want to be a jerk about this, but, uh.

Mine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey! I'm the author of this article!

Came back after a year and a half and am overjoyed to see this article hitting +60 :3

The last time I checked, it was only +12

Thanks everyone for the support!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The transition heals fatal injuries. That includes eventually-fatal injuries. If he'd stayed in Cute Animal World for another hour, he would have died.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one a lot since the re-write. Of course I'm a sucker for logs about a scientist in the process of discovery and experimentation with things that are truly bizarre. The log seems to contradict the report, though. If his back was broken but he wasn't dead, shouldn't his back have remained broken when he awoke?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it before the retouching and now I like it even more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The cakepeople, etc, may not have looked like the Prof. But he didn't check their fingerprints or DNA. What would he have found if he had? Which is more horrific - they're all him, or they're not all him?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree it shouldn't be about rape, and that wasn't what I was suggesting. Also there are several instances of creatures in the dreams that were not the Prof, so clearly other life forms exist in the dreams.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this rewrite. Last sentence a touch obvious — wish it had been some other horror — but not enough to kill it. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lovely rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ha. Amusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
………. uh. I'm going to ask you, once, to please not make this SCP about rape. Thank you.

(plus it'd be impossible for her to be impregnated by anything in her own dreamland anyway, because they'd all be genetically female regardless of phenotype.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice idea! It would be very creepy to have a female D-class wake up pregnant, especially if she refused to comment on what happened in the dream.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have now done a massive rewrite on this.

item.title: Re: Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded: Sorry should of sent those 2 posts I made as a reply to you. Read-em mabye you can squeeze them into the story

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If food in your stomach does come back Perhaps it would be interesting to have a d-class go a few weeks eating only what he eats during his dreams? And to connect with what voct Said mabye you could mention how he began to suffer from malnutrition because the food he was eating was lacking any minerals or vitamins not found in a human body. Like it would look like he has some lettuce or something in his stomach but it would be (on a microscopic level) made up of DNA and stuff matching his body

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good idea but you left out what constitutes what comes back with you. Does it need to be physically touching you or could it simply be in your pocket? If I ate food there would it remain in my stomach when I returned? Furthermore you should probably adress how he was able to breathe on a planet made entirely of candy with no plants to provide oxygen.

Oh and
(hereafter referred to as “the subject”)
They would be called scp-1587-1 not "the subject"

item.title: Re: Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded: Go for it.

item.title: Re: Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded: For this one, I definitely know what I'm going to do. Metalsonic already gave me permission; can I have until Saturday?

item.title: Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded:
Beginning deletion vote at -13.

Anyone wanting to request a stay of deletion or ask for the opportunity to rewrite may respond to this post. Do not 'vote' if you are not staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have another rewrite cooking already, you gonna be able to handle two?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Permission granted

that's gonna be super spooky and awesome

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm pretty sure I can fix this, although I'm tired right now because I was up late writing a Wikipedia article and waiting for new year's.

Part of it will include fucking around with the parameters of what exactly this thing does and can do. It still involves dreamland, yes, but… hmm.

Humans. Only humans.

I'm thinking that all the humans you meet in your dreams are, ultimately, you. If it's human, it can get back onto the bed with you and come back with you if you're not careful.

On the bed, they find a severed forearm-and-hand, holding the gun that shot the bullets that killed him. DNA shows it's his arm-and-hand, 100% match. Except he's clearly got both arms and hands.

Do I have permission to rewrite this to implement all sorts of interesting stuff?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't find it particularly interesting either, unfortunately.

item.title: Re: Wonderful twist
item.content:encoded: I didn't find it to be much of a twist, honestly. Seconding Crayne's assessment.

item.title: Re: Wonderful twist
item.content:encoded: I got that. It just really didn't make much of an impression on me, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea of being able to transport yourself to your dreams, take anything from there and bring it back to the real world. Jesus that explains why he died too, he transported the bullets from the dreamworld that where inside his chest to the real world…

Aside from that, it's well written and it did keep drawing my attention 'til the end, I really wanted to know from where did those bullets come.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was what I was trying to mean. Alternate reality gps also doesn't send the same signals as earth

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
GPS devices attached onto the subject will be temporarily disabled; however audio, visual and other recording devices retain their functionality.

SCP-1587 transports the subject and itself into an alternate dimension

If it's being transported to an alternate dimension, the GPS devices don't need to be deactivated. They'll simply be unable to get any signals, and as a result won't be able to determine the location. (Unless the alternate reality also has its own GPS satellites)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this needs work. I've attempted to do this via Addendum B, but it may still need more work

item.title: Re: Wonderful twist
item.content:encoded:
How did the guy die? When the bed doesn't allow you to get harmed?

Addendum A says he died with bullets in his chest, but with no visible gunshot wounds.

Now look at Addendum C. It says he went to watch a WW2 movie.

He watched it. He got drunk(slightly), went to bed on that, had a nightmare about WW2, and got gunned down.

Remember that you could bring back items from the dream?

His wounds were healed, and the bullet wounds were stuck in his chest.

An unfortunate loophole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't help but feel it doesn't really capture the whimsicality of dreams enough, also the death at the end doesn't do anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If the subject isn't harmed physically, how did the first person die?

Upvoted anyway because alternate universes and dream travel are fun, but some polish would help.

Perhaps you could show more of the psychological damage hinted at in the description. Those two dreams and that last one can't be the only ones. There's a lot of potential horror and distress to be had as his subconscious comes out and gets twisted.

item.title: Re: Wonderful twist
item.content:encoded: You're welcome to explain, either here or in a PM. :)

item.title: Wonderful twist
item.content:encoded: There was a twist in the addenda, but I'm not sure if you understood it or if it is good enough for you :p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted. I like the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a rating module for you. Honestly, this is boring. I read the article, hoping for some kind of wonderful twist in the addenda, but you just went took the obvious route with the letter. -1
